page: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dalsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. Multi-core performance is average, with stress-ng multi-core and passmark extended instructions falling into the middle 50%. Memory performance is also moderate, with read and write bandwidth benchmarks scoring in the average tier. The system shows strong capabilities in cryptographic and physics processing, scoring in the top 25% for PassMark CPU encryption and physics tests. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves strong performance in text generation and prompt processing for small and medium-sized language models, but drops to average tiers when handling larger 70B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32als_v7 offers a specialized hardware profile that minimizes memory overhead to optimize compute density. By limiting RAM to 2.0 GB per core and omitting local storage, it reduces resource costs for workloads that are CPU-bound rather than memory-intensive. This configuration is well-suited for compute-heavy tasks such as single-threaded data processing, lightweight LLM inference, physics simulations, and cryptographic operations. However, the low memory-to-CPU ratio makes it less suitable for large in-memory databases or memory-intensive ETL pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is a compute-focused x86_64 server from the Dalsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong cryptographic and physics processing capabilities, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads, making it suitable for tasks like single-threaded data processing, physics simulations, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is a compute-focused x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, and 64 GB RAM. It offers top-tier single-core performance for compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor optimized for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A compute-focused architecture featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and optimized memory density for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.5 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Strong cryptographic, physics, and small-model LLM inference capabilities
No local storage or integrated GPUs, optimized for external premium storage
Best suited for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads and simulations
